text.title: survey-design Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for survey-design as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Leading question: "Do you like our product?"
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Positive framing biases toward agreement; rewrite as "How would you rate your experience with [product]?" on a 1-5 scale.

failure-mode.description: Hypothetical: "How often will you use this feature?"
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Predicts nothing; rewrite as "How often have you used similar features in other tools?"

failure-mode.description: LLM drafts a Likert 5 scale by default for every attitude question.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use 7-point for nuanced attitude measurement where you need to detect small shifts; binary for behavioral self-report where ambiguity is the failure mode.

failure-mode.description: Agent summarises open-end responses with top-line themes and drops disconfirming quotes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require quote-with-respondent-ID grounding for every theme claim; disconfirming quotes are the most valuable signal in qualitative open-ends.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates MaxDiff with unbalanced item-to-set assignment.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: MaxDiff requires balanced incomplete block design; unbalanced assignment produces biased utilities. Use Conjointly or Sawtooth, not agent-generated task lists.

failure-mode.description: Agent reports "NPS went up 3 points" without acknowledging the confidence interval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Gate any NPS reporting through a stats checker tool; LLM-only NPS commentary is almost always underpowered and overconfident.
